title: Senate passes tax reform bill
text: The Senate passed a major tax reform bill on Wednesday with a 51-49 vote. The legislation, which includes cuts to corporate and individual tax rates, now heads to the House for final approval before being signed into law. Economists have offered mixed assessments of the bill's long-term impact on economic growth and the national deficit.
subject: politicsNews

title: NASA confirms water ice on Moon's surface
text: Scientists at NASA confirmed the presence of water ice on the Moon's surface at the permanently shadowed polar regions. The discovery, published in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, was made using data from the Moon Mineralogy Mapper instrument aboard India's Chandrayaan-1 spacecraft. The findings have significant implications for future lunar exploration missions.
subject: sciTech

title: Federal Reserve raises interest rates
text: The Federal Reserve raised its benchmark interest rate by a quarter of a percentage point, the third such increase this year, citing continued strength in the labor market and rising inflation. Fed Chair Jerome Powell said the central bank would continue to monitor economic data and adjust policy accordingly. Markets responded positively to the decision.

title: WHO declares end to Ebola outbreak in Congo
text: The World Health Organization declared the end of an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo after 42 days passed with no new confirmed cases. The outbreak, which began in May, resulted in 54 confirmed and probable cases and 33 deaths. Health officials praised the swift response by local and international health workers.
subject: worldnews

title: Apple reports record quarterly earnings
text: Apple Inc. reported record quarterly revenue of $88.3 billion, driven by strong iPhone sales and growth in its services division. Chief Executive Tim Cook said the results reflected strong demand across all product categories and geographies. The company also announced an increase in its share buyback program.
